meta.title: poly

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 3 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: poly
root.mentor_note: poly rarely means a lot; it means more than one, precisely.

attempt.direction_prompt: Fluent in one language, or several?

context_sentences: The hotel’s concierge was a natural polyglot, switching between five languages without seeming to notice.
Growing up near three borders had made her a polyglot before she ever opened a textbook.
The architect’s sketch showed the plaza as an irregular polygon, wider on one side than the other.
Each tile on the floor was a small polygon, cut so precisely that no two edges left a gap.
Their grandfather, a genuine polymath, could discuss law, botany and astronomy with equal ease.
The university remembers her as a polymath whose lectures wandered happily from physics into poetry.
The exam’s final question asked students to factor a polynomial with four separate terms.
Fitting a simple polynomial to the data explained the trend far better than a straight line did.

construct_options.text: A mathematical expression made of several terms added or subtracted together
A single number used repeatedly throughout a calculation
A geometric shape with many equal sides

context_sentences: The anthropologist’s book examined how polygamy was practised and regulated across different societies.
Local law had outlawed polygamy decades earlier, though the older generation still remembered it openly.

construct_options.text: The practice of having more than one spouse at the same time
A large, extended family living under one roof
A ceremony held to mark a wedding anniversary
